description: Jira Software Data Center REST API v2 (Agile v1.0 for boards/sprints). Server-only; Cloud-specific endpoints (api/3/search/jql, search/approximate-count) are NOT backported on this instance. Bearer PAT auth via PasswordSafe. All calls go through JiraApiClient.

endpoints.summary: Server version, build number and deployment type. Used for version detection at connection setup.

verdict.classification: R-INV: inventoried, not yet adopted in plugin code
verdict.reasoning: Confirmed 200 on DC 10.3.16. Plugin does not call this endpoint today — version detection happens ad-hoc. Useful for settings validation and future version-gating.

gotchas: deploymentType must be 'Server' (not 'Cloud') to confirm DC context before using DC-only endpoints

endpoints.summary: Connection check and current-user identity. Returns name, key, emailAddress, timeZone, groups, applicationRoles.
gotchas: When auth token expires, Jira responds 302 to /login.jsp and with followRedirects=true the plugin receives HTML on 200. JiraApiClient.checkJsonContentType guards against this by mapping text/html responses to AUTH_FAILED.

endpoints.summary: Extended current-user info including group memberships and application roles. Used for onboarding banner role display.

params.name: expand
params.description: Must be 'groups,applicationRoles' to populate those fields

gotchas: groups.items is empty in the probe response even though groups.size=14; items are only populated when the user has VIEW_USER_DATA or admin rights
applicationRoles.items may also be empty when the token lacks admin scope

endpoints.summary: Global permission flags for the connected user. Used to gate UI buttons (Transition, Comment, Log Work, Watch) before the user clicks.

params.name: projectKey
params.description: When provided, returns project-scoped permissions instead of global. Confirmed working on DC 10.3.16.

gotchas: Response contains both deprecated keys (e.g. EDIT_ISSUE) and modern keys (EDIT_ISSUES). Read modern keys; ignore entries where deprecatedKey==true to avoid double-counting.
Cache per project for 5 minutes to avoid hammering the endpoint on every panel render

endpoints.summary: Fetch a single issue. Used with expand=issuelinks for the basic ticket fetch (Start Work, validation).

params.name: expand
params.description: Comma-separated expand list. 'issuelinks' is used for the basic fetch; 'renderedFields' for PR context; 'changelog' for history feed.

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated field list to limit response size.

gotchas: issuelinks are not populated unless expand=issuelinks is passed
The issue numeric id (used by dev-status API) is at top-level 'id' field, not the alphanumeric 'key'

endpoints.summary: Rich issue fetch with rendered fields for PR context and commit message generation. Uses fields whitelist + expand=renderedFields.

params.name: expand
params.description: renderedFields gives description as HTML (easier to strip to plain text than wiki markup)

gotchas: renderedFields.description is HTML; strip tags before feeding to LLM
comment.comments[] is already populated in this response — no separate comment fetch needed when using this variant

endpoints.summary: Combined rich fetch + changelog in one call. Used for detail panel to get fields, rendered description, comments, and change history in a single round-trip.

params.name: expand
params.description: Both renderedFields and changelog in one call confirmed working on DC 10.3.16 (bundle-audit-followup probe E)

gotchas: changelog.histories[].items[] contains field-level changes with 'field', 'from', 'to', 'fromString', 'toString' keys
Saves two round-trips vs separate rich fetch + getComments + getChangelog calls

endpoints.summary: Available workflow transitions for an issue, with optional field schema for the transition screen.

params.name: expand
params.description: Use 'transitions.fields' to include field schema (required fields, allowed values, autoCompleteUrl) for each transition screen

gotchas: autoCompleteUrl values in field metadata point back to the same Jira host — JiraApiClient.getRawString has a same-origin guard to prevent token exfiltration via adversarial autoCompleteUrl
Required fields vary per transition and per workflow admin configuration; always check transitions[].fields[x].required before sending a transition with fields={}
60-second cache in TicketTransitionServiceImpl — stale after admin workflow changes

endpoints.summary: Execute a workflow transition (change issue status). Accepts optional fields and inline comment.
endpoints.requestBody: JSON: {"transition":{"id":"<transitionId>"}, "fields":{<fieldId>:<value>,...}, "update":{"comment":[{"add":{"body":"<wiki-markup>"}}]}}. The 'fields' and 'update' keys are optional when the transition screen has no required fields.
gotchas: Returns 204 No Content on success — no response body
400 body contains errorMessages[] + errors{} — JiraApiClient.parseJiraErrorMessage extracts user-readable text
BranchingService.startWork sends fields={} which silently 400s when admin has marked a field required on the In Progress transition (write-ops audit finding P1)

endpoints.summary: List comments on an issue, ordered by most-recent first.

params.name: orderBy
params.description: Sort order. Use '-created' for newest first.

endpoints.provenance: JiraApiClient.kt:505-513; probe Result_Jira/bundle.unpacked/summary.md row for issue_comments
gotchas: When using getIssueWithContextAndChangelog, comments are already in fields.comment.comments[] — no separate call needed

endpoints.summary: Add a comment to an issue. Body is wiki markup. Optional visibility block restricts to a role or group.
endpoints.requestBody: JSON: {"body":"<wiki-markup-text>", "visibility":{"type":"role"|"group","value":"<name>"}}. The visibility block must be OMITTED ENTIRELY (not set to null) when no restriction is wanted — Jira rejects visibility:null.
gotchas: QuickCommentPanel and JiraCommentPanel currently send no visibility block — all comments are unrestricted even when team policy requires role-restricted closure comments (write-ops audit P1)
DC uses [~username] for @mentions, NOT @username (Slack style) and NOT [~accountId:...] (Cloud style)

endpoints.summary: List worklogs for an issue.
endpoints.provenance: JiraApiClient.kt:562-566; probe Result_Jira/bundle.unpacked/summary.md row for issue_worklogs

endpoints.summary: Log time worked on an issue. Accepts timeSpent in Jira format (e.g. '2h 30m'), optional comment, optional started date, and optional estimate adjustment.

params.name: adjustEstimate
params.description: How to adjust remaining estimate: 'auto' (server default), 'new', 'manual', 'leave'

endpoints.requestBody: JSON: {"timeSpent":"2h 30m", "comment":"<text>", "started":"yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSZ"}. 'comment' and 'started' are optional. Server defaults started to 'now' when absent.
gotchas: TimeLogPanel collects a date field but currently discards it (write-ops audit P1) — the server silently defaults started to now
adjustEstimate=new requires additional newEstimate query param; adjustEstimate=manual requires reduceBy query param
timeSpent must be Jira canonical format 'Nh Mm' — invalid formats like '2hrs' return 400

endpoints.summary: External links attached to an issue. Returns Confluence backlinks and generic external URLs.
gotchas: application.type == 'com.atlassian.confluence' identifies Confluence backlinks vs generic external URLs
Returns empty array [] when no remote links are attached — not 404

endpoints.summary: List watchers on an issue. isWatching flag indicates if the current user is watching.
Add current user (or named user) as a watcher. Body is a bare JSON string (the username as a JSON-quoted scalar).
endpoints.requestBody: A bare JSON string literal — the username quoted as JSON, e.g. "jdoe". This is the documented Jira DC API shape; NOT a JSON object.
gotchas: Body must be a raw JSON string (e.g. "jdoe"), not {"username":"jdoe"}
Returns 204 No Content on success
The watch toggle UI exists in TicketDetailPanel but currently does not invoke addWatcher/removeWatcher (write-ops audit: NOT-EXPOSED-AS-UI)

endpoints.summary: Remove a watcher from an issue. Username passed as query param.

params.name: username
params.description: Username of the watcher to remove

endpoints.summary: Fetch a single custom field value for an issue. Used for acceptance-criteria custom field lookup.
JQL issue search (GET variant). Used by searchIssues, searchByJql, searchByJqlPaged.
gotchas: GET URL length limit applies — JQL with 100+ keys (key IN (...)) exceeds URL limits; use POST /rest/api/2/search for batch validation
searchIssues caps input at MAX_SEARCH_TEXT_LENGTH=500 chars and rejects control characters

endpoints.summary: JQL issue search (POST variant). Used by validateTicketKeys to avoid URL-length limits with large IN-clause batches.
endpoints.requestBody: JSON: {"jql":"key in (PROJ-1,PROJ-2,...)", "fields":["summary","status"], "maxResults":100}. Same response shape as GET /rest/api/2/search.
gotchas: Exactly same JSON response shape as GET — existing JiraIssueSearchResult parser works unchanged
Keys must match anchored regex ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]+-\d+$ before JQL interpolation to prevent JQL injection via joinToString
POST /rest/api/3/search/jql (Cloud cursor pagination) returns 302 on this DC — NOT backported; stay on v2

endpoints.summary: History-grouped issue suggestions for @-mention input. Returns sections with matching issues grouped by search type.

params.name: query
params.description: Search term — can be key-prefix (e.g. 'PROJ-') or free text

gotchas: For key-prefix queries (e.g. 'PROJ3-'), only 'History Search' section appears — 'Current Search' section does NOT appear
Use summaryText (plain text) for display labels, not keyHtml which contains <b> markup
Replaces the former manual board+sprint walking in MentionSearchProvider (R-SWAP-2)

endpoints.summary: User's favourite saved JQL filters. Used to populate the Saved Filters section in the Sprint tab.
gotchas: GET /rest/api/2/filter/search returned 404 on this DC — only favourite filters are available, not arbitrary filter search
Filter name values are not redacted in probe output (redactor leak) — treat as potentially containing PII

endpoints.summary: Single saved filter detail including its full JQL. Used when user clicks a favourite filter to execute its query.
gotchas: The 'jql' field is the migration target — feed it into the existing searchIssues(jql) after user clicks
GET /rest/api/2/filter/search (search by name) returned 404 on this DC — favourites list is the only discovery mechanism

endpoints.summary: Search saved filters by name. Returns 404 on Jira DC 10.3.16 — not backported from Cloud.

verdict.reasoning: Probed and received 404. Cloud-only. Plugin must use /filter/favourite instead.

endpoints.summary: Fast approximate JQL hit count without fetching results. Returns 404 on Jira DC 10.3.16 — Jira 10 native endpoint, not backported.

verdict.reasoning: Probed and received 404. Would have been useful for Sprint tab badge counters. Not available on this instance.

endpoints.summary: Modern cursor-based JQL search (Cloud-style). Returns 302 redirect to login on Jira DC 10.3.16.

verdict.reasoning: Probed and received 302 → /login.jsp?permissionViolation=true. Not backported to this DC instance. Plugin must remain on v2 search.

endpoints.summary: List boards. Supports optional type filter (scrum/kanban) and name filter. Used by Sprint tab board picker.

params.name: maxResults
params.description: Max results per page. Plugin uses 200.

gotchas: Response is paginated (isLast flag); total=6691 on the probed instance — plugin uses maxResults=200 which may not return all boards for large enterprise instances

endpoints.summary: List sprints for a board, filtered by state. Used for active and closed sprint fetching.
Issues in a sprint. JQL filter applied server-side; plugin uses assignee=currentUser() by default.

params.name: jql
params.description: Additional JQL filter applied within the sprint scope

endpoints.summary: Issues on a Kanban board (no active sprint). JQL filter applied server-side; plugin uses resolution=Unresolved AND assignee=currentUser() by default.

Atlassian-internal dev-status API. Returns development data (branches, pull requests, commits, builds, deployments, reviews) for an issue by numeric ID. Powers the Dev Status section in the plugin's ticket detail panel.

params.name: issueId
params.description: Numeric Jira issue ID (NOT the alphanumeric key). Read from issue.id field.

params.name: applicationType
params.description: Integration type. Use 'stash' for Bitbucket Data Center.

endpoints.provenance: probe Result_Jira/bundle.unpacked/raw/devstatus_branch.json (and devstatus_pullrequest, devstatus_repository, devstatus_build, devstatus_deployment, devstatus_review); JiraApiClient.kt:486-499 and 573-603

verdict.classification: R-INV: internal Atlassian API, not officially supported
verdict.reasoning: All 6 dataType variants probed and returned 200. Stable since Jira 7.x, powers Jira's own Dev Panel. No public deprecation policy — treat as high-risk if this ever returns 404.

gotchas: INTERNAL API — no public deprecation policy; no SLA from Atlassian if removed in a future DC version
issueId must be the numeric ID from issue.id (e.g. '3825349'), NOT the alphanumeric key (e.g. 'PROJ-030')
applicationType must be 'stash' for Bitbucket DC — 'bitbucket' (Cloud) would be different
Empty detail[] array is a valid response (no linked dev data), not an error
getDevStatusBranches delegates to this endpoint; getDevStatusPullRequests/Commits/Builds/Deployments/Reviews also use it with different dataType values

endpoints.summary: Search for users assignable to a specific issue. Used in transition dialog assignee picker.

params.name: issueKey
params.description: Issue key to scope assignable user search

params.name: query
params.description: Partial username or display name to search

gotchas: Accepts both 'query' and 'username' params unlike generic /user/search (which requires 'username' on DC)

endpoints.summary: Generic user search. On Data Center requires 'username' param (NOT 'query'). The plugin had a bug using query= which returned 400.

params.name: username
params.description: Username prefix to search. DC requires 'username' param; 'query' param returns 400 on Server.

endpoints.sampleResponse: {"errorMessages":["The username query parameter was not provided"],"errors":{}}

verdict.classification: R-FIX-1: DC requires username= param, not query=
verdict.reasoning: Probe confirmed 400 with query=a. DC /user/search requires username= parameter. Cloud-only syntax uses query=. Bug in JiraSearchServiceImpl.searchUsers was renamed in the implementation commit.

gotchas: DC Server requires 'username' query param — 'query' param (Cloud style) returns 400 with 'The username query parameter was not provided'
Use /user/assignable/search (which accepts both) when searching within issue context

endpoints.summary: Search for groups. Used in transition dialog for group-type fields.
All fix versions for a project. Cached 5 minutes in plugin. Used in transition dialog version picker.
All components for a project. Cached 5 minutes in plugin. Used in transition dialog component picker.
List project roles for a project. Returns a name-keyed object; role ID parseable from the URL tail. Used to populate comment visibility combo.
gotchas: Returns a JSON object keyed by role name, NOT an array: {"Developers":"https://jira.host/rest/api/2/project/PROJ/role/10001", ...}
Role ID is parsed from the URL tail (last path segment after /role/)

endpoints.summary: All fields (system + custom). Used for acceptance-criteria custom field discovery to replace manual customfield_10001 config.
gotchas: Returns a flat array of all fields. The 'custom' boolean distinguishes system vs custom fields.
Cache 5 minutes per settings page load to avoid repeated calls

endpoints.summary: Label autocomplete suggestions. Internal Atlassian endpoint — code has a 404 fallback that returns empty list, degrading to free-form input.

verdict.classification: R-INV: internal endpoint, stable since Jira 7.x but unsupported
verdict.reasoning: Probed 200 on DC 10.3.16. Code already has 404 → empty-list fallback which is the correct defensive pattern.

gotchas: INTERNAL endpoint under /rest/api/1.0/ — no public deprecation policy
Must handle 404 gracefully (return empty list) since this endpoint can disappear without notice
